procedure_name: CT for Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia - Maintenance
source_files: Guidance document for processing PM-JAY packages for Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: DM (Medical oncology) or equivalent (MD Medicine with experience/Fellowship in Hematology/Medical Oncology)
clinical_indications: Standard risk patients in complete remission after induction therapy, to be given for 2 years
High-risk patients in complete remission and no prospect for allogeneic stem cell transplant for 2 years

clinical_thresholds.note: Bone marrow report showing remission (if not repeated post-induction)
Patient must be in remission for maintenance therapy

preauth.label: Documentation of induction dates (start and end date along with the name of regimen)
Documentation of consolidation dates (start and end date along with the name of regimen)
Remission status – BM report with blast percentage (if in remission post induction it need not be repeated) and date of bone marrow report

claim.label: Reports of the tests (pathology, radiology, microbiology, hematology, biochemistry, etc.)
Discharge summary of inpatient department and/or day care and/or injection room
Copy of treatment protocol with dates and doses which were delivered at the end of each month

ppd_preauth.q: Bone marrow report for maintenance package is not available or not in remission?
Patient is elderly, frail, or has multiple comorbidities (contraindication for procedure)?
Patient is not in remission (contraindication for maintenance)?

cpd_claim.q: Reports of all relevant tests uploaded?
Copy of treatment protocol with dates and doses delivered at the end of each month uploaded?

common_queries: Missing documentation of induction or consolidation dates/regimen for maintenance pre-authorization.
Remission status (BM report) for maintenance not uploaded or not showing remission.
Patient is elderly, frail, or with multiple comorbidities (contraindication for intensive treatment).
Patient is not in remission (contraindication for maintenance).
Maintenance package re-claimed beyond the 1st line regimen restriction.
Missing drug barcodes or treatment protocol copy at claim submission.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Essential Medical Personnel: Qualified Medical Oncologist/Hematologist; Essential Medical Infrastructure: Chemotherapy unit (Day care/Injection room/Ward)
clinical_key_pointers: ALL classification (B or T cell ALL) based on bone marrow or peripheral blood examination.
Risk stratification considers age, WBC count, and cytogenetics analysis.
NCG clinical guidelines for Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia are to be followed.
Packages are once in lifetime and as a 1st line regimen; same protocol cannot be repeated.
additional_information.post_op_care: Mandatory parameters to be monitored - CBC, RFT, LFT and infections; Follow-up care: OPD visits, CBC, LFT, RFT, peripheral smear after treatment completion
contraindications: Elderly and frail with multiple comorbidities
